case_name: Francisco Perez, Jr., Individually, and by Laura Aguirre his natural guardian and next friend, and Laura Aguirre, Individually v. Michael Hu, M.D. St. Catherine Hospital of East Chicago Indiana, Inc.

summary.brief: Francisco Perez, Jr., Individually, and by Laura Aguirre his natural guardian and next friend, and Laura Aguirre, Individually v. Michael Hu, M.D. St. Catherine Hospital of East Chicago Indiana, Inc.. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Aguirre was diagnosed with gestational diabetes and was treated for it. Gestational diabetes increases the risk of having a significantly larger than average, or macrosomic, baby.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Aguirre qualified as obese during her pregnancy. A c-section substantially reduces the risk of shoulder dystocia during birth.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Aguirre, who has an elementary school education and does not speak English, does not recall Dr. Hu ever discussing the increased risk of harm to her baby during a vaginal birth as opposed to a c-section.
evidence_index.speaker: Aguirre's testimony

evidence_index.text: Prior to her labor being induced, Aguirre signed a consent to treatment form, specifying that she was consenting to a vaginal delivery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On February 8, 2010, Aguirre gave birth to Francisco. During the delivery, shoulder dystocia occurred.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Halbridge went on to testify that, given Aguirre’s risk factors, Dr. Hu should have advised her of the risk of shoulder dystocia and resulting brachial plexus injury occurring during vaginal birth and the reduced risk of such injury during a c-section.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Halbridge

evidence_index.text: Therefore, the trial court erroneously granted Dr. Hu’s motion for directed verdict on the issue of informed consent. We reverse and remand for further proceedings on that issue.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: Elementary school education, does not speak English

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient is seven months pregnant with gestational diabetes and obesity. Previous three children were born vaginally without complications.
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Pregnancy with risk factors for shoulder dystocia

requestables.name: Consent for delivery method
requestables.description: Discussion of risks and benefits of vaginal delivery versus c-section

reveal.result_summary: Patient signed consent for vaginal delivery, but risks of shoulder dystocia not adequately discussed
reveal.decision_impact: Patient was not fully informed of risks associated with vaginal delivery

key_events.event: Consent form signed for vaginal delivery

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with gestational diabetes and macrosomic baby
decision_points.prompt: What delivery method should be recommended?

options.clinical_reasoning: C-section reduces risk of shoulder dystocia and brachial plexus injury
Vaginal delivery chosen without adequate risk discussion

expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated high risk of shoulder dystocia

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to adequately inform patient of risks associated with vaginal delivery
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been advised of the risks and offered a c-section
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Francisco suffered brachial plexus injury resulting in permanent disability of left arm

key_findings: Directed verdict in favor of Dr. Hu on informed consent issue
Jury found in favor of Dr. Hu on negligence claim

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to adequately inform patient of risks
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: C-section could have reduced risk of injury

diagnoses.diagnosis: Shoulder dystocia with brachial plexus injury

treatments.treatment: Vaginal delivery with additional pressure applied

court_findings.finding: Directed verdict in favor of Dr. Hu on informed consent issue
